LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT SUMMIT REAL ESTATE GROUP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFF ROCKY POINT PARTNERS, LLC IS DENIED. "WHERE THE WRITING AT ISSUE SHOWS 'NO MORE THAN AN INTENT TO FURTHER REDUCE THE INFORMAL WRITING TO A MORE FORMAL ONE' THE FAILURE TO FOLLOW IT WITH A MORE FORMAL WRITING DOES NOT NEGATE THE EXISTENCE OF THE PRIOR CONTRACT'.HOWEVER, WHERE THE WRITING SHOWS IT WAS NOT INTENDED TO BE BINDING UNTIL A FORMAL WRITTEN CONTRACT IS EXECUTED, THERE IS NO CONTRACT." (HARRIS V. RUDIN, RICHMAN & APPEL (1999) 74 CAL.APP.4TH 299, 307.) IN THIS CASE, THE HANDWRITTEN DOCUMENT AND CONFLICTING E-MAILS CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE REGARDING THE PARTIES' INTENTIONS AND WHETHER THE HANDWRITTEN DOCUMENT IS A BINDING CONTRACT. "[T]HE ULTIMATE RESOLUTION OF THE PARTIES' INTENT IS A FACTUAL ISSUE'" (RAM'S GATE WINERY, LLC V. ROCHE (2015) 235 CAL.APP.4TH 1071, 1083.) PREVAILING PARTY MUST SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER TRACKING THE COURT'S RULING VERBATIM AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR #59761, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-08-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-04-2016 Continued To Oct-11-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Plaintiff Rocky Point Partners, Llc
Off Calendar Sep-08-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Ray Yetka of Utrecht & Lenvin LLP (415-357-0600) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Abram P. Petersen of Lubin Olson & Niewiadomski (415-981-0550) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT SUMMIT REAL ESTATE GROUP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Plaintiff Rocky Point Partners, LLC is DENIED. "Where the writing at issue shows 'no more than an intent to further reduce the informal writing to a more formal one' the failure to follow it with a more formal writing does not negate the existence of the prior contract'.However, where the writing shows it was not intended to be binding until a formal written contract is executed, there is no contract." (Harris v. Rudin, Richman & Appel (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 299, 307.) In this case, the handwritten document and conflicting e-mails create a triable issue regarding the parties' intentions and whether the handwritten document is a binding contract. "[T]he ultimate resolution of the parties' intent is a factual issue'" (Ram's Gate Winery, LLC v. Roche (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 1071, 1083.) Prevailing party must submit a [proposed] written order tracking the Court's ruling verbatim and in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #59761, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-08-2016 Continued To Apr-06-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. RAY YETKA, ESQ. of Utrecht & Lenvin, LLP, Tel No. 415-357-0600 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. ABRAM P. PETERSEN, ESQ. of Lubin Olson & Niewiadomski LLP, Tel No. 415-981-0550 appearing for the Defendant Summit Real Estate Group, Inc.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT SUMMIT REAL ESTATE GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, Summit's demurrer to the sole cause of action for breach of contract is overruled with twenty days to answer. The breach of contract claim is not contradicted or otherwise barred by the purported contract itself. On a demurrer, the court is required to accept plaintiff's interpretation of the contract as long as it is plausible, which it is, and that interpretation is contrary to defendant's position that the purported contract is an unenforceable agreement to agree/ Plaintiff has adequately alleged both performance of the contract by plaintiff's assignor and its right to sue as an assignee. Summit has no right to enforce the provision of the assignment contract that the assignor must sue in its own name, even assuming that that provision even applies. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
